findings.evidence: Grep hits show the new lemmas lack attributes at `AbelJacobiFiniteSum.lean:52`, `:62`, `:73`, `:85`, `:95`, `:105`; the analogous public normal forms are `@[simp]`, e.g. `AbelJacobiSum.lean:93` for `weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass_ofPoint`, `FiniteSum.lean:223` for `degree_ofFinsupp`, `:504` for `degree_ofFinsetWithMultiplicity`, and `:700` for `degree_ofFinset`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the six lemmas at lines 52, 62, 73, 85, 95, and 105; their right-hand sides contain only sums of point Abel-Jacobi classes, so these are terminating constructor-normalization rules.
findings.issue: All six characteristic normal forms for `weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass`/`unweightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass` on `ofFinsupp`, `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity`, and `ofFinset` are plain lemmas, so `simp` will not rewrite Abel-Jacobi sums of the public finite-divisor constructors to the advertised finite sums.

summary: The finite Abel-Jacobi constructor normal forms are exposed but not registered as simplification lemmas, so the intended downstream finite-effective-divisor API does not normalize by automation.
